tavily.answer: Somalia is experiencing severe drought conditions, with 2026 potentially being the worst year yet, leading to significant humanitarian crises. International aid has drastically decreased, with funding slashed from $2.38 billion in 2022 to $531 million, causing agencies to scale back lifesaving programs. The drought has caused rivers to dry up, crops to fail, and pastoral families to lose their herds. Additionally, Somalia faces a high risk of famine, with parts of the country experiencing acute malnutrition levels not seen since 2022. The decline in aid and the impact of global cuts to foreign assistance are complicating efforts to address food shortages and ongoing insecurity.
